tex:
Gabriela designs the seating layout for a new theatre.
\newline
There are three sections of seats, A, B and C.
\newline

(a) Section A has 152 seats.
\newline
Section B has 12.5\% more seats than Section A.
\newline
Section C has $\dfrac{3}{8}$ of the number of seats in Section A.
\newline

(i) (b) Show that the number of seats in Section B is 171.
\newline

(c) Show that the total number of seats is 380.
\newline

(d) Write down and simplify the ratio of the number of seats in each section A : B : C.
\newline

A : B : C =  :  : 

(e) In Section A:

There are 12 seats in the front row.
\newline

Each row has 2 more seats than the row in front of it.
\newline

Work out the number of rows for the 152 seats in Section A.
\newline

 rows

(f) For a concert in the theatre, the ticket prices are in the ratio
A : B : C = 9 : 7 : 4.
\newline
A ticket for Section C costs \$6.
\newline

(i) (g) Show that a ticket for Section B costs \$10.50.
\newline

(h) Find the cost of a ticket for Section A.
\newline

\$ 

(i) The table shows the number of tickets sold in each section.
\newline
Section

Number of tickets sold

A

120

B

136

C

30

Calculate the total amount received from the ticket sales.
\newline

\$ 

(j) The concert costs \$4500 to organise.
\newline
Calculate the amount received from the ticket sales as a percentage of the \$4500.
\newline

 \%

_rawBody:
Gabriela designs the seating layout for a new theatre.
There are three sections of seats, A, B and C.

\begin{parts}
\item Section A has 152 seats.
Section B has 12.5\% more seats than Section A.
Section C has $\dfrac{3}{8}$ of the number of seats in Section A.

\begin{subparts}
\item Show that the number of seats in Section B is 171.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Show that the total number of seats is 380.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{2}

\end{subparts}

\item Write down and simplify the ratio of the number of seats in each section A : B : C.

A : B : C = \AnswerLine[] : \AnswerLine[] : \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item In Section A:

There are 12 seats in the front row.

Each row has 2 more seats than the row in front of it.

Work out the number of rows for the 152 seats in Section A.

\AnswerLine[] rows
\Marks{2}

\item For a concert in the theatre, the ticket prices are in the ratio
A : B : C = 9 : 7 : 4.
A ticket for Section C costs \$6.

\begin{subparts}
\item Show that a ticket for Section B costs \$10.50.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Find the cost of a ticket for Section A.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{1}

\item The table shows the number of tickets sold in each section.
Section

Number of tickets sold

A

120

B

136

C

30

Calculate the total amount received from the ticket sales.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\item The concert costs \$4500 to organise.
Calculate the amount received from the ticket sales as a percentage of the \$4500.

\AnswerLine[] \%
\Marks{1}

\end{subparts}
\end{parts}

 $^\circ$C
